title: The Donner Party
summary: A wagon train was trapped by winter, and survival pressure pushed the group toward starvation, breakdown, and rescue failure.
whyWild: The event becomes a brutal test of leadership, trust, and what people will sacrifice when the trip stops being a journey and becomes a trap.
stakes: Life, moral compromise, and whether desperation can erase the line between necessity and collapse.

title: Apollo 13
summary: An oxygen tank explosion turned a lunar mission into a desperate improvised return flight.
whyWild: The story is a precision-engineering disaster where calm teamwork is the only thing keeping everyone alive.
stakes: Survival, technical competence, and whether discipline can beat catastrophe.
hinge: Whether improvisation can defeat a system failure.

title: The Trojan Horse
summary: A hidden deception inside a gift becomes the strategy that ends a war.
whyWild: It is a perfect story engine for betrayal, infiltration, and the cost of trusting the wrong symbol.
stakes: Victory, trust, and whether intelligence beats force.
hinge: Whether deception is a legitimate weapon or a moral fracture.

title: The Great Emu War
summary: Soldiers were deployed against emus, and the campaign became a famous absurd failure.
whyWild: It reads like satire, but it is real enough to become a brilliant source for ironic war stories and doomed bureaucracy.
stakes: Authority, pride, and the gap between official power and actual control.
hinge: Whether competence exists on paper or in the field.

title: The Mary Celeste
summary: A merchant ship was found adrift with no clear explanation for why the crew vanished.
whyWild: It is a clean mystery box: a believable setting, an empty vessel, and enough uncertainty to fuel endless speculation.
stakes: Safety, trust, and the terror of a solved-looking problem that is not solved at all.
hinge: Whether the absence of evidence is itself the story.

title: The Dyatlov Pass Incident
summary: Nine hikers died under unexplained and disturbing circumstances during a winter expedition.
whyWild: The event sits right on the edge between survival story, mystery, and cosmic dread.
stakes: Human survival, hidden causes, and the fear that the real explanation may be worse than the rumor.
hinge: Whether the truth is mundane or horrifying.

title: The Franklin Expedition
summary: Two ships vanished while trying to chart the Northwest Passage, turning exploration into tragedy.
whyWild: It is a lost expedition story with enough historical weight to support horror, survival, or imperial collapse narratives.
stakes: Discovery, empire, and the cost of trying to map the impossible.

title: The Tunguska Event
summary: A massive explosion flattened a huge forest area, and the cause became a long-running mystery.
whyWild: It has the scale of catastrophe without the clean explanation, which makes it perfect for cosmic or speculative remixing.
stakes: Safety, knowledge, and what happens when a world-changing event has no clear witness story.
hinge: Whether the unknown is a scientific problem or a narrative one.

title: The Roswell Incident
summary: A crashed object triggered military secrecy, civilian speculation, and decades of UAP debate.
whyWild: It is a ready-made seed for secrecy, mistrust, and stories about how institutions handle the unexplained.
stakes: Truth, authority, and the line between evidence and mythology.
hinge: Whether secrecy protects reality or creates legend.

title: The Dancing Plague of 1518
summary: Dozens of people reportedly danced for days in a public outbreak that still feels strange and half-understood.
whyWild: It sits at the border of social panic, mass psychosis, and historical weirdness, which makes it a strong engine for horror or allegory.
stakes: Body, mind, crowd behavior, and whether a community can absorb a pressure it cannot name.
hinge: Whether a strange event is supernatural, social, or both.
